checks_run: python3 -c "import ast; ast.parse(...)" -> AST_PARSE_OK (test_mctp_tx_assembler.py is syntactically valid Python). Offline oracle self-test REFERENCE_MODEL_SELFTEST_OK (fragment ceil(len/MTU), SOM/EOM, seq mod4, DSP0236 header byte3 0x8D/0x6D, body order, zero_len/oversize/underrun -> no packets). SEQ_WRAP_ORACLE_OK: with CSR MTU=16 and a legal 96-byte body (<= MAX_MSG default 192), ceil(96/16)=6 packets give seq [0,1,2,3,0,1] -- a genuine mod-4 3->0 wrap on a non-oversize message. SCOREBOARD_SEQNUM_SCHEMA_OK: the refined SCN_TX_SEQNUM emits 6 per-packet rows + 1 summary row, all carrying the 10 scoreboard_rows.v1 fields, expected_source=cycle_rules.tx.seqnum, coverage_refs=[COV_TX_SEQNUM_WRAP], observed mod4_wrap_3to0=true. GENERAL_SCHEMA_OK: shared Scoreboard emits all 10 scoreboard_rows.v1 fields PLUS event_id; expected_source in {behavior_model,cycle_rules}, observed_source has a valid kind in {monitor,dut_signal} AND a DUT-facing signal locator; passing row empty mismatch / failing row non-empty mismatch; coverage_refs resolve to per-objective COV_TX_* goals; 12 SCN_TX_* tests present. ROW_SHAPE_OK (closure-format fixes A+B): every row carries event_id = the EVT_TX_* for its scenario (INTAKE/FRAGMENT/SOM_EOM/SEQNUM/HEADER/SINGLEMSG/STATUS/RESET -> matching EVT, all four SCN_TX_ERR_* -> EVT_TX_ERR), and observed_source is a dict {kind + signal-locator} distinct from expected_source (egress rows -> monitor signal m_valid,m_ready,m_data,m_sop,m_eop; reset/status/error rows -> dut_signal naming irq_done/irq_err/prdata(<regs>)/m_* actually sampled). ROW_SHAPE_OK (closure-format fix C): every row now also carries top-level contract_id = CONTRACT_TX_<X> and obligation_id = OBL_TX_<X> per scenario (all four SCN_TX_ERR_* -> CONTRACT_TX_ERR / OBL_TX_ERR), in addition to event_id, goal_id, and the 10 required fields; self-test verified contract_id+obligation_id+event_id+10 fields on every row. COVERAGE_REFS_OK: passing rows use exactly the COV ids {COV_TX_INTAKE, COV_TX_FRAGMENT_BOUNDARIES, COV_TX_SOM_EOM, COV_TX_SEQNUM_WRAP, COV_TX_HEADER_FIELDS, COV_TX_SINGLEMSG, COV_TX_ERR_MATRIX, COV_TX_STATUS, COV_TX_RESET}; failing rows carry an empty coverage_refs (failed checks cannot contribute to closure coverage). ORACLE_UNCHANGED_OK: reference model, expected values, pass/fail logic, and scenario coverage are byte-for-byte unchanged (seq [0,1,2,3,0,1]; header byte3 0x8D/0x6D; zero_len/oversize/underrun -> no packets). Full simulation NOT run (sim-execution agent owns sim/; RTL not yet present at dispatch time).

architecture_roles.driver: drive_message + APB-lite apb_write/apb_read + configure; ingress s_* and CSR stimulus only
architecture_roles.monitor: EgressMonitor samples DUT-facing m_valid&m_ready / m_data / m_sop / m_eop and decodes packets (header + payload + SOM/EOM/SEQ); observation only
architecture_roles.predictor: ReferenceModel (independent Python oracle) encoding behavior_model.tx.fragment/som_eom/header/err and cycle_rules.tx.seqnum; never reads DUT
architecture_roles.scoreboard: Scoreboard emits scoreboard_rows.v1 JSONL to ../sim/scoreboard_events.jsonl, one row per checked item
architecture_roles.coverage: coverage_refs per row map to verification_plan per-objective COV_TX_* goals
architecture_roles.result_writer: scoreboard JSONL written at run time; results.xml/scenario_mapping.json produced by cocotb/sim-execution agent

tb_methodology_notes.stimulus_strategy: directed micro-TB; legal-message scenarios + negative error scenarios (underrun via s_abort mid-message, oversize via len>MAX_MSG, zero_len via s_empty&s_last) + backpressure via m_ready toggling; no constrained-random (profile random disabled)
tb_methodology_notes.coverage_strategy: contract-linked per-objective coverage refs recorded for passing checks; failed rows excluded from closure coverage by policy
tb_methodology_notes.assertion_hooks: none added (directed scoreboard is byte-exact and sufficient for v0; assertion/formal candidates remain in verification_plan for a later stronger-proof pass)
design_truth_notes: SCN_TX_SEQNUM: a genuine mod-4 wrap (seq 3->0) needs >=5 packets. Under default MTU=64 and MAX_MSG=192 the deepest legal message is only 3 packets (seq 0,1,2), so the wrap would be unreachable. RESOLVED per team-lead guidance with no ontology/constant change: the test programs a SMALL MTU via the CSR (MTU=16) and drives a fully legal body (96 bytes <= MAX_MSG=192), giving ceil(96/16)=6 packets => seq 0,1,2,3,0,1. The 3->0 mod-4 wrap is now demonstrated end-to-end on a non-oversize message; one scoreboard_rows.v1 row is emitted per packet plus a summary row confirming the observed wrap, all with expected_source=cycle_rules.tx.seqnum and coverage_refs=[COV_TX_SEQNUM_WRAP]. Expected seq still comes only from cycle_rules.tx.seqnum, never the DUT. MAX_MSG stays at its default in this scenario; oversize behavior is exercised only in SCN_TX_ERR_OVERSIZE.

summary: Implemented the cocotb directed micro-TB for mctp_tx_assembler as a black-box against the frozen interface (doc/v0_parameters_interface.md). Built an independent Python ReferenceModel (predictor) that computes the expected MCTP packet byte stream and error outcome purely from input body + attributes (dest_eid/msg_tag/to) + CSR config (src_eid/MTU/MAX_MSG), encoding behavior_model.tx.* and cycle_rules.tx.seqnum; expected values never come from DUT output. Separate driver (ingress s_* + APB-lite CSR + backpressure) and EgressMonitor (DUT-facing m_* sampling and packet decode). Twelve directed tests cover all 12 SCN_TX_* scenarios including the three negative error scenarios (underrun/oversize/zero_len -> ERR_FLAGS bit, DROP_CNT++, irq_err, NO egress packet) and backpressure (no byte lost). Scoreboard writes scoreboard_rows.v1 JSONL to ../sim/scoreboard_events.jsonl with the 10 required fields, per-scenario expected_source matching evidence_plan, DUT-facing observed_source, and contract-linked coverage_refs. Three closure-format fixes were applied per team-lead guidance (emission shape only; no change to expected values, the reference model, pass/fail logic, or scenario coverage): (A) every row now carries an event_id field set to the scenario's EVT_TX_* (all four SCN_TX_ERR_* share EVT_TX_ERR) so the closure check can resolve each contract's scoreboard_row_ref; (B) every observed_source is now a dict carrying a valid kind AND a DUT-facing signal locator (egress rows -> monitor with m_valid,m_ready,m_data,m_sop,m_eop; reset/status/error rows -> dut_signal naming the irq_done/irq_err/prdata(<regs>)/m_* signals actually sampled), kept distinct from expected_source (behavior_model/cycle_rules); a defensive guard in Scoreboard.record injects a locator if a caller omits one; (C) every row now also carries top-level contract_id=CONTRACT_TX_<X> and obligation_id=OBL_TX_<X> per scenario (all four SCN_TX_ERR_* -> CONTRACT_TX_ERR / OBL_TX_ERR) for TB_CHECK_SCOREBOARD_ROWS_HAVE_CONTRACT_AND_OBLIGATION. Passing rows carry exactly the COV_TX_* coverage_refs (now defined in tb_methodology.yaml); failing rows carry empty coverage_refs so a failed check cannot contribute to closure coverage. SCN_TX_SEQNUM was refined per team-lead guidance to prove a real mod-4 wrap: with CSR MTU=16 and a legal 96-byte body (<= MAX_MSG default 192) it produces 6 packets seq 0,1,2,3,0,1, emits one scoreboard row per packet plus a summary row confirming the observed 3->0 wrap, all expected_source=cycle_rules.tx.seqnum, coverage_refs=[COV_TX_SEQNUM_WRAP]; the earlier MAX_MSG-raise workaround was removed (MAX_MSG behavior now lives only in SCN_TX_ERR_OVERSIZE). tb/Makefile targets SIM=icarus with -g2012. AST parse, oracle self-tests (incl. SEQ_WRAP_ORACLE_OK), and scoreboard schema self-tests (incl. SCOREBOARD_SEQNUM_SCHEMA_OK and GENERAL_SCHEMA_OK with 12 tests) all pass; full simulation deferred to the sim-execution agent (owns sim/; RTL not present at dispatch time).
